# Tests — what is needed before we can ship?

**Verdict:** ship-with-caution — the offline test layer is genuinely green and trustworthy (444 total / 431 pass / 0 fail / 13 skip, `tsc --noEmit` clean, reproduced this session), but it proves only the offline logic; nothing run by default proves the real on-device stack (Chromium-backed gate, Ollama, Docling) or that the packaged macOS artifact works, and there is no CI/release gate of any kind. Execute the named release checklist green before tagging.

> **✅ Remediation (2026-06-15):** offline suite now **491 / 466 / 0 / 25 skip** (+35
> tests). Added: a `verify` script + committed `githooks/pre-push` (the release gate
> that was missing); `src/templates/audit.test.ts` runs all 8 templates (+ themed +
> no-levels rubric + styled shell) through the REAL axe auditor — which **caught a real
> badge-withholding bug** (`document-title` missing from the Canvas shell, now fixed);
> `e2e/packaged-smoke.test.ts` launches the packaged `.app`; bundled-Chromium resolver
> unit-tested; new units for mutex / alignment / sidecar-vision / daemon-crash /
> RCDATA-RAWTEXT / renderer-DOM innerHTML-sink + a `describe_image` size guard. The
> P0 release-gating items are now *runnable* (see [RELEASING.md](../RELEASING.md)); the
> only remaining work is executing steps 2 + 5 on arm64 hardware. See
> [SHIP-READINESS.md](./SHIP-READINESS.md#-remediation-status-2026-06-15--all-8-conditions-addressed).

## Summary

The inner-loop posture is solid: the offline e2e (`e2e/full-turn.test.ts:50-108`) drives the **real** unconditional gate (`enforceGate`) over both the badge-granted and badge-withheld paths with only the browser and sidecars faked, and the two units PRD §10 flagged as "internals not reviewed" — rubric-ID routing and the accessible `ThemeResolver` — actually have strong behavior-locking tests. So this pillar has **no code defects or regressions**. Every confirmed gap is either (a) at the integration/packaging boundary that the default suite deliberately does not touch, or (b) missing-test debt on currently-correct, low-risk, often already-deferred code. Under the single-user on-device threat model the gate is fail-safe (`A11Y_FAIL_OPEN=false`), so the worst case of an untested path is a withheld badge or a self-inflicted hang — not a false WCAG certification. The real ship risk is that "tests pass" today does **not** imply "the shipped app works," and there is no automated gate enforcing even the offline baseline.

## Confirmed findings

Ordered by threat-model-adjusted severity. All are CONFIRMED in code; one medium, the rest low/info.

| Sev | Finding | Location |
|-----|---------|----------|
| **medium** | Production output gate depends on a Chromium binary packaging never bundles, and no green-by-default test launches the real auditor. The prod auditor (`createAuditor(playwrightRunner)`) calls `chromium.launch()` with **no** `executablePath`, so it resolves from the global ms-playwright cache; `files` packs only `dist/**`, `extraResources` bundles only the two sidecars. A packaged DMG would have no browser, so every BUILD/Remediate turn errors (fail-closed, no false cert). Compounded: the 6 real-auditor tests are skip-by-default. | `src/engine/render/playwright-runner.ts:150-153`; `src/engine/render/index.ts:19`; `src/orchestrator/gate.ts:72`; `src/runtime/app-api.ts:371`; `package.json:38-55`; `src/engine/render/integration.test.ts:16` |
| low | The 8 BUILD templates are validated only against the allowlist, never through the real axe `audit()` the gate enforces. `render.test.ts` imports `validateAllowlist` only (line 5), never `audit`; `deps.test.ts:64-75` is allowlist-only. Heading structure and rubric `th scope` ARE asserted via regex, and themed contrast is AA-guaranteed upstream by `resolveTheme`, so the untested surface is the axe pass as a whole + the degenerate no-levels rubric branch. Fail-safe at runtime. | `src/templates/render.test.ts:91-152`; `src/templates/templates.ts:307-313`; `src/orchestrator/gate.ts:70-92` |
| low | All 13 env-gated tests skip by default and are wired to no release gate; the 6 browser tests are the *only* exercise of the real Chromium+axe auditor, the 7 LLM/Docling tests the *only* exercise of the real sidecars. The default suite proves offline logic, not that the external stack works. | `src/engine/render/integration.test.ts:16`; `src/llm/integration.test.ts:30-31`; `e2e/live.test.ts:49-92`; `package.json` `test`/`e2e` scripts |
| low | No CI runner or pipeline of any kind — confirmed no `.github/`, `.gitlab-ci.yml`, `Jenkinsfile`, `.husky/`, or `Makefile`; `package.json scripts` has no aggregate verify/release target. Nothing automatically enforces typecheck+tests before a commit or tag. | repo root; `package.json` scripts (`typecheck`/`build`/`test`/`e2e`/`app`/`llm:smoke` only) |
| low | No smoke test that the packaged `.app` launches and exposes the preload bridge. `check-bridge.mjs`/`drive-app.mjs` drive `electron .` from the dev `dist/` tree (main = `dist/app/main.js`), never a packaged artifact, so asar/path/preload/resourcesPath breakage would ship undetected. | `scripts/check-bridge.mjs:7`; `scripts/drive-app.mjs:14-19`; `scripts/probe-runtime.mjs` |
| low | electron-builder mac config references files/dirs that don't exist — the DMG build cannot run as configured. `extraResources.from` points at `resources/sidecars/ollama` + `docling-serve` and entitlements at `build-resources/entitlements.mac.plist`; none of `resources/`, `build-resources/`, `release/` exist on disk (confirmed). The certain hard blocker is the missing `extraResources.from` sources; entitlements only matter at the (cert-gated) signing step. | `package.json:44-55,64-65`; filesystem |
| low | Mutex (LLM serialization invariant) has zero direct tests — no `src/llm/mutex.test.ts`. Neither the FIFO/mutual-exclusion guarantee nor the deferred no-timeout leaked-lock failure mode is locked, so a refactor could silently break serialization (risking a second model copy in unified memory) with no RED signal. Note: a throw does *not* leak the lock (`mutex.run`'s `finally` releases); the real wedge vector is a never-resolving/abandoned stream since `acquire()` has no timeout. | `src/llm/mutex.ts:10-18`; consumed at `src/llm/sidecar.ts:37,62,67` |
| low | Daemon-crash / no-respawn behavior is uncharacterized — the only `process.test.ts` test is the ENOENT spawn-failure reject. No test emits `'exit'`, asserts `isHealthy()` flips, or pins the clean post-crash failure. No RED scaffold exists for the deferred respawn supervisor. (Char-test should assert child-nulled, not an `isOwned` flip — `owned` stays true after exit.) | `src/llm/process.ts:100-103,62-76`; `src/llm/process.test.ts:17-22` |
| low | No RED/characterization test proves a prompt-injected "WCAG 2.2 AA certified" string in `view.text` reaches the UI even when the badge is withheld. `enforceGate` runs only over extracted HTML fragments; `view.text` is taken verbatim from the model (`text: turn.text`) and never gated. The verbatim pass-through is *locked* (`app-api.test.ts:94`), but the deferred prose-injection gap has no test to invert when a frozen TurnView/scrubber lands. Badge — the real trust signal — is correctly withheld. | `src/runtime/app-api.ts:377-378,447-448`; `src/runtime/app-api.test.ts:94` |
| low | SB5 Course Alignment Coach is entirely untested, including the pure, trivially-testable `composeAlignmentPrompt`. The per-fragment "Check alignment" reuse (`renderer.ts:62`) passes only content, exercising both inferred-placeholder branches — untested. Near-zero-cost lock missing on a named product subsystem. | `src/app/renderer/alignment.ts:19-42,53-102`; `src/app/renderer/renderer.ts:21,62,104` |
| low | All five renderer panels (Guidance/transcript, brand-kit, sessions, alignment, preview/export) have zero tests and no DOM-faking harness exists. Two XSS-adjacent invariants are correct but unverified at the DOM level: `previewFrame()` sets `sandbox=''` + `srcdoc=previewSrcdoc(html)` (`preview.ts:33-34`) and the remediate `before` is written via `textContent` while `gatedBody` is the sole `innerHTML` sink (`conversation.ts:64,88`). A future regression here would turn pasted hostile HTML into script execution with no test to catch it. (Note: the *pure* `previewSrcdoc` IS covered by `canvas-shell.test.ts:20-22`; only the DOM-bound wiring is unverified.) | `src/app/renderer/{conversation,brandkit,sessions,alignment,preview}.ts`; `src/app/renderer/ui.ts:57-58` |
| low | `describe_image` vision path has no sidecar-level test — no `src/llm/sidecar.test.ts`. The `LLM_VISION_ENABLED=false` throw and the role-mapped two-part multimodal message assembly in the real `OllamaSidecar.describeImage` are exercised only by the default-skipped integration test (which never calls it). Mitigating: the downstream transform IS covered (`payload.test.ts:10,15,19-28`), the toggle parse IS covered (`config.test.ts:42-43`), and the sync-throw→rejection path is correct (`tools.ts:37`). It's a local feature flag, not a privacy/exfiltration boundary. | `src/llm/sidecar.ts:100-117`; `src/runtime/deps.ts:121` |
| low | `describe_image` has no image-size / token-budget guard at all (PRD §10) — and no test because there is no guard. `opts.image` flows verbatim into the chat payload (`flattenContent` appends with no length cap). Bounded by a 120s request timeout and `num_predict` for the model-tool path; the only fully-unguarded path is a direct programmatic call with a user blob — self-inflicted on a single-user device (worst case: hang/OOM on the user's own GPU). Add the guard first (RED), then test. | `src/llm/sidecar.ts:104-116`; `src/llm/payload.ts:80-90` |
| low | RCDATA/RAWTEXT divergence for `textarea`/`title`/`noscript`/`xmp`/`plaintext` is unmodeled and uncharacterized. `RAW_TEXT_TAGS = {'script','style'}` only, so the other five are tokenized as normal markup (diverges from Chromium/Canvas). No characterization test pins today's behavior, so a future tokenizer change could shift it silently. LATENT — neutralized in prod by the sandboxed iframe + CSP + Canvas save-time sanitizer, and the allowlist strips dangerous attributes regardless. Fidelity gap, not an escape. | `src/engine/allowlist.ts:126,328-346`; `allowlist.test.ts` (script raw-text only, :243-248) |
| info | **Healthy:** rubric-ID retrieval routing (`RUBRIC_ID_PATTERN` + exact-unit routing + case-insensitivity + unknown-id FTS fallback) and the accessible `ThemeResolver` (every resolved pair passes AA, low-contrast warnings, determinism, invalid-color rejection) are well covered — *not* the gaps PRD §10 implies for those specific units. Aim coverage effort at the gaps above, not here. | `src/knowledge/retriever.test.ts:53-59,140-160`; `src/theme/theme.test.ts:31-117` |
| info | **Healthy:** offline suite + strict typecheck are green and the offline e2e exercises the real gate. Reproduced this session: `npm test` → 444/431/0/13 in ~1.3s; `tsc --noEmit` exit 0. `e2e/full-turn.test.ts:50-108` drives the real `enforceGate` over both badge-granted and badge-withheld paths with only the browser/sidecars faked. Necessary-but-not-sufficient — pair with the integration+packaging+smoke gates before any tag. | run-verified; `e2e/full-turn.test.ts:50-108` |

## Checked & cleared / refuted

- **Launch-per-fragment ScanRunner reuse-safety test (REFUTED → info).** Claimed there's no statelessness/leak test protecting a future single-Chromium refactor. Refuted as a meaningful gap: the launch-per-fragment design is stateless and leak-free *by construction* (fresh browser per call + unconditional `finally { browser.close() }` at `playwright-runner.ts:191-193`), there is no current defect, and `render/integration.test.ts:19` already shares one runner across six different fragments — implicitly characterizing the "repeated independent calls" contract a reuse refactor would need to preserve. The only net-new value is an explicit "throwing fragment still closes the browser" assertion — marginal, and only relevant *if/when* the deferred SB1 work is undertaken.
- **Sidecars spawned from PATH, not bundled resourcesPath (REFUTED on HIGH framing → low).** The code observation is accurate (both sidecars spawn bare `ollama`/`docling-serve` with no PATH augmentation), but the "documented mechanism contradicted / packaged app will ENOENT" framing does not hold: `README.md:99-100` lists resourcesPath resolution explicitly under "What the lead must add before packaging (out of scope to run here)" — an acknowledged pending task, not a current guarantee. Packaging has never been executed, the dev run path resolves via the inherited shell PATH, and an ENOENT today degrades *honestly* via `createUnavailableApi` (never fabricates a passing badge). It's a legitimate pre-ship packaging item, rated low.
- **Worth restating for trust:** the env-gated skips are an *intentional, documented* gating choice (each integration file header explains how to run it), not flakiness; the gate's *enforcement logic* IS unit-tested by default (`src/orchestrator/gate.test.ts`) with stub auditors — only its real-Chromium backing is gated; and the dev-only esbuild HIGH (npm audit) never ships in prod deps (only axe-core + playwright ship), so it is out of scope for this pillar.

## Action items before ship

Explicit test plan. Effort: trivial / cheap / medium. "Blocker?" = blocks a *release tag*, not the offline inner loop.

**P0 — release-gating (do before any tag)**

1. **Real-auditor + sidecar release gate** — *blocker.* Make `RUN_BROWSER_INTEGRATION=1 tsx --test src/engine/render/integration.test.ts` green on target hardware (proves the Chromium+axe engine behind every badge), plus `RUN_OLLAMA_INTEGRATION=1` + `RUN_DOCLING_INTEGRATION=1` `e2e/live.test.ts` green. Assertion: real audits return fragments + badges; sidecars answer. Effort: cheap (tests exist; just run them and wire to the checklist).
2. **Bundle Chromium + assert it** — *blocker.* File: packaging config + `src/engine/render/playwright-runner.ts`. Install with `PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH=0` (or add ms-playwright to `extraResources`) and set `launchOptions.executablePath` from `process.resourcesPath`. Test: a packaged-app smoke test runs one real `audit_html` turn and asserts a gated fragment + badge. Effort: medium.
3. **Packaged-`.app` smoke test** — *blocker.* File: new `e2e/packaged-smoke` (Playwright launching the built `.app`). Assertion: `window.canvasAgent` bridge loads, `#health` resolves, one `render_template`+audit turn returns a gated fragment. Effort: medium.
4. **Pre-package path-existence + electron-builder gate** — *blocker.* File: new `scripts/pre-release.*`. Assertion: every path in the build config (`extraResources.from`, entitlements, buildResources) exists and is non-empty; then `electron-builder --mac` succeeds. Populate `resources/sidecars/*` + `build-resources/entitlements.mac.plist` (or remove unused entries). Effort: medium.
5. **`verify` script + pre-push/pre-tag hook** — *blocker (process).* File: `package.json` `"verify": "tsc --noEmit && npm test"` + a committed hook, ideally a self-hosted arm64 CI job running the full checklist. Effort: cheap.

**P1 — close the highest-value coverage gaps (cheap, do soon)**

6. **`src/templates/audit.test.ts`** — for each of the 8 `TemplateType` fixtures (incl. a themed assignment and the **no-levels** rubric path at `templates.ts:307-313`): `const issues = await audit(renderTemplate(...).html)`; assert no `blocker`/`error` issue. Locks the core "BUILD produces badge-passing artifacts" promise against the same allow+axe pair the gate runs. Effort: medium.
7. **`src/llm/mutex.test.ts`** — (a) two `run()` calls; assert the second body starts only after the first resolves (record start/end markers); assert release idempotency. (b) `acquire()` once without release, then race a second `acquire()` against a short delay and assert it has NOT settled (documents the no-timeout wedge; flip to `assert.rejects` when a timeout lands). Effort: trivial.
8. **`src/app/renderer/alignment.test.ts`** — pure `composeAlignmentPrompt`: provided objectives+rubric (trimmed, correctly labeled); omitted → both placeholders; whitespace-only → placeholder; content trimming. Effort: trivial.
9. **`src/llm/sidecar.test.ts`** — injected `FetchLike`+env: `LLM_VISION_ENABLED=false` → `describeImage` rejects and fetch is never called; enabled → captured body has role mapped to vision model and `messages[0].images = [stripped base64]`. Effort: cheap.

**P2 — characterization / deferred-item scaffolding (cheap, nice-to-have)**

10. **Renderer DOM harness + `preview.test.ts` / `conversation.test.ts` / `sessions.test.ts` / `brandkit.test.ts`** — stand up a tiny fake `document`/`window` honoring `ui.ts`. Assert `previewFrame` sets `sandbox=''` and `srcdoc===previewSrcdoc(html)` and never writes `innerHTML`; assert `before` via `textContent` with `gatedBody` the sole `innerHTML` sink; happy-path CRUD wiring vs a fake AppApi. Effort: medium.
11. **`app-api.test.ts` prose-conformance characterization** — script a turn returning "This page is WCAG 2.2 AA certified." + a blocker-triggering fragment; assert `frag.gate.badgeWithheld===true` AND (today) `view.text` still contains the phrase. Invert when a frozen TurnView/prose scrubber lands. Effort: cheap.
12. **`process.test.ts` daemon-crash characterization** — fake `EventEmitter` child via the injected `SpawnLike`; drive to owned/healthy, emit `'exit'`; assert observed-down (child nulled) and NO auto-respawn today. Flip to assert auto-recovery when the supervisor lands. Effort: cheap.
13. **`allowlist.test.ts` RCDATA/RAWTEXT characterization** — for `textarea`/`title`/`noscript`/`xmp`/`plaintext`, pin today's output and assert no executable attribute survives the allowlist regardless. Tighten when RAWTEXT/RCDATA modeling is added. Effort: cheap.
14. **`describe_image` size guard (RED→GREEN)** — add a max-decoded-bytes guard in `describeImage`/`payload`, then test: oversized image rejects before any fetch; in-budget passes. Effort: medium.
